page: Amazon Web Services c6i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 virtual CPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 2.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 92,898.99 and a PassMark CPU score of 75,255.86. The processor's cryptographic capabilities are highlighted by a top-tier PassMark CPU encryption test score of 86,799.68, aided by hardware-accelerated instructions such as AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, reaching 320.22 GB/sec for uncached large block reads and 135.64 GB/sec for large block writes. Single-core performance sits in the average tier, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 1,651.0.

Given its compute-heavy profile and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the c6i.24xlarge is designed for CPU-bound workloads. It demonstrates strong capabilities in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations. It is also highly capable of handling high-throughput static web serving and Redis SET operations, which reached 85.93 million operations per second. The lack of local storage and GPUs makes it less suitable for heavy local data caching or deep learning training, but it offers a resource-dense configuration for parallel processing, high-performance computing, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services c6i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, with no local storage or GPUs. The instance delivers strong multi-core performance, highlighted by a PassMark CPU score of 75,255.86 and a top-tier encryption score of 86,799.68. It also features a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps. This resource-dense configuration is highly efficient for CPU-intensive tasks, including software compilation, multi-threaded database operations, high-throughput static web serving, and parallel processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong multi-core performance for parallel processing workloads.

tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual server featuring ninety-six virtual CPUs and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for demanding parallel workloads.

192.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero local storage or GPU resources
Strong multi-core and cryptographic performance with hardware-accelerated encryption
Best suited for software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving
